facts.text: In 1788, the First Fleet founded Sydney as a British penal colony, naming the cove Sydney Cove after Home Secretary Thomas Townshend.
Sydney's 2000 Summer Olympics spurred the construction of world‑class venues, including the Sydney Olympic Park and the Sydney Motorsport Park in Eastern Creek.
La Perouse, a suburb named after French navigator Lapérouse, hosts the historic Bare Island military outpost and Botany Bay National Park.
Darlinghurst, once a remote settlement, now shelters the historic Darlinghurst Gaol, a former convict prison turned cultural landmark.
In 1814, Governor Macquarie commissioned convict Francis Greenway to design the iconic Macquarie Lighthouse, Sydney's first lighthouse.
